chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The topic is Disjoint sets. In this video I will cover the following things.

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First is what are disjoint sets and the operations on them. Then detecting a cycle. See disjoint

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: sets are useful for detecting a cycle in non-directed graph or undirected graph. So we

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: will take an example and learn how to detect a cycle in a non-directed graph. Then graphically

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: how to represent these disjoint sets and how it can be represented using array. Lastly the

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: time efficient operations on disjoint sets are weighted union and collapsing find that are based

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on the ranks or weights. Disjoint sets are similar to sets topic in mathematics but not exactly.

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: They are little bit changed for making them useful in algorithms. The famous algorithm that

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: uses disjoint set is Kruskal's algorithm which detects a cycle in a graph. Let us see how these

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: disjoint sets are different from normal sets of mathematics. Let us understand what are disjoint

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari

chunks.text: graph two components I have taken and each component I have represented it as a set.

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: so that is disjoint. Disjoint set means two sets are not having anything common.

chunks.title: Disjoint Sets Data Structure - Weighted Union and Collapsing Find - Abdul Bari

text: The topic is Disjoint sets. In this video I will cover the following things. First is what are disjoint sets and the operations on them. Then detecting a cycle. See disjoint sets are useful for detecting a cycle in non-directed graph or undirected graph. So we will take an example and learn how to detect a cycle in a non-directed graph. Then graphically how to represent these disjoint sets and how it can be represented using array. Lastly the time efficient operations on disjoint sets are weighted union and collapsing find that are based on the ranks or weights. Disjoint sets are similar to sets topic in mathematics but not exactly. They are little bit changed for making them useful in algorithms. The famous algorithm that uses disjoint set is Kruskal's algorithm which detects a cycle in a graph. Let us see how these disjoint sets are different from normal sets of mathematics. Let us understand what are disjoint sets. Here I have a non-connected and non-directed graph. There are two components it's not connected graph two components I have taken and each component I have represented it as a set. Set S1 is having 1,2,3,4 these are the vertices of this component and set 2 is having 5,6,7,8 these are the vertices of second component. The two different components are representing two different sets and what is a disjoint here? No vertex is common here the numbers are not common here. If you take intersection of these two S1 and S2 then you will not get anything. 5 empty set so that is disjoint. Disjoint set means two sets are not having anything common. Then how we use them what are the operations on them? We prefer performing only two operations that are find and union. I will show what does it mean by these operations. First operation find I want to know vertex 5 belongs to which set? It's not here it's here so it belongs to set 2. Find operation is to find out any element or a vertex belonging to which set. It's like a set membership operation also means that element is a member of which set. I want to find out 7, doesn't belong here, here it belongs so it belongs to set 2. Then 3, find 3 where it is?
